name: OpenClaw Memory Max
description: SOTA Memory Suite: Auto-Recall Hooks, Cross-Encoder Reranking, Multi-Hop Deep Search, Semantic Causal Graph, Episodic Memory, Utility Vectors, and Nightly Sleep-Cycle Consolidation.

enableRulePinning.description: Enable YAML rule pinning from MEMORY.md into the system prompt. Off by default — must be explicitly opted in. Only enable if you control write access to MEMORY.md.

enableAutoCapture.description: Enable automatic capture of high-value user messages (rules, corrections, preferences) to sidecar files. Off by default — opt in if you want persistent message logging.

enableAutoRecall.description: Enable automatic memory injection before each agent turn via lifecycle hooks.

OPENCLAW_HOME.description: Base directory for OpenClaw data. Defaults to ~/.openclaw if not set.

reads: $OPENCLAW_HOME/main.sqlite — Read-only access to the memory database (chunks table for search)
$OPENCLAW_HOME/openclaw.json — Read plugin config from the global config file
$OPENCLAW_HOME/memory/MEMORY.md — Read YAML rule blocks (only when enableRulePinning=true)
writes: $OPENCLAW_HOME/memory/utility_scores.json — Reinforcement scores per memory ID (~100 KB)

privacy.modifiesSystemPrompt: auto-recall injects <relevant-memories> into session context via hook (not global config); rule pinning is opt-in (enableRulePinning=false by default) and also injected via hook only
privacy.dataRetention: sidecar files auto-truncated after 30 days by in-process sleep cycle
